meta.note: Full sample stories with craft-annotation layer. Each = {id, title, question, paragraphs:[{text, note}]}. 'note' is the craft x-ray tip for that paragraph (shown when annotation toggle is ON). Leave note empty for no annotation. Fill with your own stories.

paragraphs.text: It was a grey, restless evening in late October when I finally decided to leave. The platform was almost empty, and a cold wind carried the smell of rain and iron along the tracks.
paragraphs.note: SCENE: notice the atmosphere adjectives (grey, restless, cold) and the sensory detail (smell of rain and iron) — the mood is set before anything happens.

paragraphs.text: As the minutes crawled by, I kept checking the departure board, my hands refusing to stay still. Part of me still hoped someone would appear and tell me to stay.
paragraphs.note: DEVELOP: 'show-don't-tell' — 'hands refusing to stay still' shows nerves without naming them. The time phrase 'as the minutes crawled by' sequences the action.

paragraphs.text: Suddenly, the announcement crackled overhead. This was it. My heart hammered as I stepped towards the open door, gripping my single suitcase.
paragraphs.note: TENSION: the interrupter 'Suddenly' breaks the rhythm; vivid verbs (hammered, gripping) and the short sentence 'This was it.' sharpen the turning point.

paragraphs.text: Looking back now, I realise that stepping onto that train changed everything. I had been terrified — but it was the bravest thing I have ever done.
paragraphs.note: RESOLUTION: the closing time-marker 'Looking back now' signals reflection; the story resolves on feeling and meaning, not just events.
[craft note: what technique is at work here]
